'''George Carmont''' (born 30 June 1978), also known by the [[nickname]] of '''"Chicken George"''' or '''"Gorgeous George"''',<ref name=comeback/> is a former [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|ce}} for the [[Newcastle Knights]] in the [[National Rugby League]] (NRL) and the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Super League]].

==Early life==
Carmont was born in [[Auckland]], New Zealand. He was educated at [[De La Salle College, Mangere East]].

==Club career==
===Youth career===
Carmont's junior club was the [[Otahuhu Leopards]] in the [[Auckland Rugby League]] competition.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.warriors.kiwi/news/2015/03/20/your-team--junior-clubs-in-focus/ |title=YOUR TEAM: Junior clubs in focus |publisher=Warriors |date=20 March 2015 |access-date=31 May 2022}}</ref>

===Newcastle Knights===
Carmont made his professional rugby league début for the [[Newcastle Knights]], against the [[Melbourne Storm]] on 11 March 2004. He appeared in 83 games and scored 33 tries along with becoming a popular player with the fans.

===Wigan Warriors===
On 27 November 2007, it was announced that Carmont had signed a two-year deal with [[Super League]] club [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]].<ref name="Wigan sign Samoa centre Carmont">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/wigan/7115051.stm|title=Wigan sign Samoa centre Carmont|publisher=BBC Sport|date=2007-11-27|access-date=2007-11-27}}</ref> Carmont was named in the [[Super League Dream Team]] for 2008's [[Super League XIII]].<ref name="2008 engage Super League Dream Team">{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/article.php?id=11755|title=2008 engage Super League Dream Team|publisher=Super League|date=2008-09-15|access-date=2008-09-26|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100721195910/http://www.superleague.co.uk/article.php?id=11755|archive-date=2010-07-21}}</ref>

He played in the [[2010 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite news|last=Wilson|first=Andy|title=Wigan turn St Helens into sinners with Super League Grand Final win|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2010/oct/02/wigan-st-helens-grand-final|access-date=21 November 2019|newspaper=guardian.co.uk|date=2 October 2010}}</ref>

Carmont played for the Wigan Warriors in the [[2011 World Club Challenge]] against [[2010 NRL season|2010 NRL]] premiers, the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]], and scored two tries, but it was not enough to gain his side the victory.

2011's [[Super League XVI]] for Wigan Warriors started with a [[Magic Weekend]] match against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]], in which Carmont scored one try.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/9393298.stm|title=St Helens 16-16 Wigan|date=12 February 2011}}</ref> He followed this up with another try against the [[Bradford Bulls]] in Round 2 late in the match.<ref>{{cite news|title=Bradford 10-44 Wigan|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/9400211.stm|access-date=5 May 2011|newspaper=BBC|date=20 February 2011}}</ref> His next score was made in a loss against the [[Catalans Dragons]] in Round 8, before touching down a week later against [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in Round 9.<ref>{{cite news|title=Warriors torched by Dragons|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugbyleague/match_report/0,19936,11070_3343396,00.html|access-date=5 May 2011|newspaper=Sky Sports|date=8 April 2011|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110411011337/http://www.skysports.com/rugbyleague/match_report/0,19936,11070_3343396,00.html|archive-date=11 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|title=Hull KR 16-28 Wigan|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/9455872.stm|access-date=5 May 2011|newspaper=BBC|date=15 April 2011}}</ref>

Carmont played as a {{rlp|ce}} in the [[2011 Challenge Cup Final]] 28–18 victory over the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref>{{cite news|last=Scott|first=Ged|title=Leeds 18-28 Wigan|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/14670204|access-date=21 November 2019|newspaper=[[BBC Sport]]|date=27 August 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news| url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2011/aug/27/leeds-rhinos-wigan-warriors-challenge-cup | location=London | work=[[The Guardian]] | first=Andy | last=Wilson | title=Leeds Rhinos 18-28 Wigan Warriors – Challenge Cup final match report | date=27 August 2011}}</ref>

==International career==
Carmont was selected in the [[Samoa national rugby league team#2008 World Cup Squad|Samoa]] squad for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="Samoa, Tonga and Fiji name squads">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7658506.stm|title=Samoa, Tonga and Fiji name squads|publisher=BBC|date=2008-10-08|access-date=2008-10-09}}</ref> He appeared in three matches for Samoa and scored two tries. He is eligible for the [[United States national rugby league team]] through his American Samoan descent.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.leagueunlimited.com/article.php?newsid%3D6026|title=Tomahawks V Kangaroos |access-date=2009-08-07|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070909064253/http://www.leagueunlimited.com/article.php?newsid=6026|archive-date=2007-09-09}}</ref>

Carmont was selected for the [[Exiles rugby league team|Exiles]] squad for the [[International Origin]] match against [[England national rugby league team|England]] at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium|Headingley]] on 10 
June 2011.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/13278410|title=Brian McClennan completes Exiles squad to face England fixture|work=BBC Sport|date=4 May 2011|archive-date=13 January 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160113141331/https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/13278410|url-status=live}}</ref>

On 3 November 2011 The annual [[RLIF Awards]] dinner was held at the [[Tower of London]], and Carmont was named [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] player of the year.<ref>{{cite news|title=Slater scoops player award|url=http://www1.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/15313/7289077/Slater-scoops-player-award|access-date=5 November 2011|newspaper=[[Sky Sports]]|date=3 November 2011}}</ref>

==Later years==
Carmont trained briefly with the [[New Zealand Warriors]] in 2013, but ultimately did not join the team and retired.<ref name=comeback/>

In 2015 Carmont joined the [[New Zealand Warriors]] as an assistant coach for the [[NSW Cup]] side.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.stuff.co.nz/sport/league/10663248/Well-travelled-Carmont-joins-Warriors-reserves|title=Well-travelled Carmont joins Warriors reserves|first=David|last=Long|date=26 October 2014|via=Stuff.co.nz}}</ref> He played one game for the team during the season, scoring a try in the match.<ref name=comeback>{{cite web|url=http://www.warriors.kiwi/news/2015/03/14/nsw_cup_round_2_carm.html|title=NSW CUP ROUND #2 | Carmont's comeback - Warriors|website=Warriors.kiwi|date=14 March 2015|access-date=2016-08-21}}</ref> He again made a one-match comeback in 2016.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2016/08/20/mounties_hold_on.html|title=Mounties Hold On|website=NSWRL.com.au|date=20 August 2016|access-date=2016-08-21}}</ref>

George has now made a name for himself as a frequent visitor at Las Vegas 7s
